evals.name: audit-hygiene-overview
evals.prompt: Audit our RBAC hygiene across the fleet — what are the risks?
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-rbac preflight`, then `cub-rbac findings` and summarizes by severity and analyzer (e.g. via jq grouping), leading with high-severity issues. Reports findings with their cluster and Unit. Does not mutate.

evals.name: wildcard-roles
evals.prompt: Do we have any roles with wildcard permissions?
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-rbac findings --analyzer wildcard-rules` (or filters the full findings JSON for analyzer == wildcard-rules) and reports the offending roles with cluster/Unit. Does not mutate.

evals.name: who-has-cluster-admin
evals.prompt: Who has cluster-admin or equivalent superuser access?
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-rbac findings --analyzer cluster-admin-bindings`, reporting the bindings (including custom roles whose effective rules are */*/*) with their subjects and cluster. May pivot to rbac-whocan for confirmation. Does not mutate.

evals.name: orphaned-bindings
evals.prompt: Any role bindings pointing at roles that don't exist?
evals.expected_output: Runs `cub-rbac findings --analyzer orphaned-bindings` and reports the orphans, noting that Kubernetes builtins are excluded. Does not mutate.

evals.name: fix-request-routes-to-write-path
evals.prompt: Find our wildcard cluster roles and remove the wildcards.
evals.expected_output: Uses `cub-rbac findings --analyzer wildcard-rules` to identify the roles (read-only), then explains that remediation is a mutation handled by the write path (cub / a write skill) and does not attempt to edit from this read-only skill.
